original_question:
Joanne has a blank fair six-sided die and six stickers each displaying a different integer from 1 to 6. Joanne rolls the die and then places the sticker labeled 1 on the top face of the die. She then rolls the die again, places the sticker labeled 2 on the top face, and continues this process to place the rest of the stickers in order. If the die ever lands with a sticker already on its top face, the new sticker is placed to cover the old sticker. Let $p$ be the conditional probability that at the end of the process exactly one face has been left blank, given that all the even-numbered stickers are visible on faces of the die. Then $p$ can be written as $\tfrac mn,$ where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m + n.$

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

entries.original_incomplete_question: Olivia has a blank fair nine-sided die and nine stickers each displaying a different integer from [BLANK]. Olivia rolls the die and then places the sticker labeled 1 on the top face of the die. She then rolls the die again, places the sticker labeled 2 on the top face, and continues this process to place the rest of the stickers in order. If the die ever lands with a sticker already on its top face, the new sticker is placed to cover the old sticker. Let $p$ be the conditional probability that at the end of the process exactly one face has been left blank, given that all the even-numbered stickers are visible on faces of the die. Then $p$ can be written as \tfrac{m}{n}, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m + n.$

variants.variant: Olivia possesses a [BLANK] fair nine-sided die and nine stickers, each featuring a distinct integer from [BLANK]. She rolls the die and affixes the sticker marked 1 onto the top face of the die. Next, she rolls the die once more, places the sticker marked 2 on the top face, and continues this sequence to apply the remaining stickers in order. If at any point the die shows a sticker already on its top face, the new sticker is placed over the existing one. Let $p$ denote the conditional probability that, after completing the process, precisely one face remains blank, given that all even-numbered stickers are visible on the die's faces. Thus, $p$ can be expressed as 	frac{m}{n}, where $m$ and $n$ are coprime positive integers. Determine the sum $m + n.$
